body.title: Granny: externalize all in-memory state for clean restart
body.result: dispatched_ids persist to dispatched_cycle.json on restart; GRANNY_STATS on start/stop
body.description:
KnightlyBuilder principle: keep state external from the process so it can be restarted freely without data loss. Granny currently holds _dispatched_ids, _total_dispatched, _total_errors in memory — these evaporate on restart (as we saw during 2026-06-02 development where Granny was restarted 3x).

Already external: worker availability (files), dispatch config (granny.yaml), ticket state (Postgres via cc_queue).
Needs to be external: dispatched-this-cycle ids (flat file or Postgres, cleared on startup by reset-stale), cumulative stats (channel post on startup/shutdown so slate captures them).

Concretely: write _dispatched_ids to ~/.granny/dispatched_cycle.json on each update; read it on startup; _stats persist as a GRANNY_STATS channel post on stop/start (no DB write needed — channel is durable).

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; flat JSON file acceptable for cycle-local dedup (not critical data); stats to channel for observability
**Scope boundary:** IN: persist dispatched_ids to flat file; restore on start; post stats to channel at lifecycle events. OUT: full distributed state replication, Postgres for cycle cache
**Completion criteria:** restart Granny mid-cycle; dispatched_ids survive; duplicate dispatches prevented; GRANNY_STATS posted on start and stop
